name: GPS positioning with atmospheric corrections and quality metrics
description: Tests that the agent applies atmospheric corrections (a must-follow rule) and reports DOP/scatter quality metrics when computing a basic GPS position from RINEX data.
prompt: I have RINEX v3 observation and GPS navigation files in the Navigation Toolbox sample data directory. Compute a GPS receiver position solution and tell me how accurate it is. Use the GODS station files.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB using shipped RINEX sample data
Atmospheric corrections applied via gnssoptions — without them, vertical error exceeds 20 m
DOP values reported as quality indicators
Uses tiledlayout/nexttile for multi-panel figures instead of deprecated subplot
assertions.expected: tiledlayout used instead of subplot for any multi-panel visualization

assertions.description: Does not use subplot (skill convention: always use tiledlayout/nexttile)

name: Multi-constellation positioning with BeiDou pre-filtering
description: Tests the critical gotcha: gnssmeasurements errors if observed satellites lack matching nav data. The agent must pre-filter, and should inspect observation codes before choosing one.
prompt: Process the GODS station RINEX data using GPS, Galileo, and BeiDou together for a multi-constellation position solution. Use the shipped sample files from Navigation Toolbox.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error — notably does not crash on BeiDou missing nav data
Pre-filters BeiDou observation data to satellites with matching navigation messages using intersect or equivalent
assertions.expected: intersect on SatelliteID or equivalent filtering of obs to nav-matched satellites

assertions.description: Inspects RINEX file metadata before reading to confirm available constellations and observation codes
Chooses an observation code deliberately per constellation rather than relying on defaults for all systems
assertions.expected: Explicit observation code string passed to gnssmeasurements for at least Galileo or BeiDou

assertions.description: Atmospheric corrections applied to the combined solution
Uses a band-1 observation code for BeiDou (C1P, C1X, or any C1*) — not C2I which diverges in urban environments
assertions.expected: BeiDou gnssmeasurements call uses a band-1 code (C1P, C1X, or similar C1* code), not C2I

assertions.description: Does not use subplot — skill convention: tiledlayout/nexttile for any multi-panel figures

name: GPS-only vs multi-constellation performance comparison
description: Tests that the agent solves configurations independently and produces a quantitative comparison with DOP and scatter metrics, following the skill's iterative refinement pattern.
prompt: Using the GODS station RINEX sample data, compare GPS-only positioning accuracy against GPS+Galileo. Which configuration gives better results and by how much?

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error for both GPS-only and GPS+Galileo configurations
Solves both configurations independently so they can be compared
assertions.expected: Two separate receiverposition calls, one for GPS-only and one for GPS+Galileo

assertions.description: Reports horizontal scatter RMS or equivalent accuracy metric for comparison
Reports DOP values for both configurations to show geometry improvement
Reports satellite count — a key factor in multi-constellation benefit
assertions.expected: Number of satellites used reported or computed for each configuration

assertions.description: Does not use subplot — skill convention: tiledlayout/nexttile for any multi-panel figures

name: Elevation mask, C/N0 filtering, and visualization
description: Tests filtering workflow: C/N0 on observation timetable, elevation via lookangles, satellite count reporting, and tiledlayout visualization with labeled axes.
prompt: Using the GODS station RINEX sample data, compute a GPS position solution with a 15-degree elevation mask and 30 dB-Hz C/N0 threshold. Show me the results in a multi-panel figure and tell me how many satellites were filtered out.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error with both filters applied
C/N0 filtering applied to observation timetable via S1C column, not to measurements timetable
assertions.expected: Filtering on obsData.GPS or gpsObs using S1C column before calling gnssmeasurements

assertions.description: Uses lookangles for elevation mask filtering
C/N0 filtering uses the S1C column (skill's prescribed pattern: replace C prefix with S)
Uses tiledlayout/nexttile (not subplot) with axis labels including units and panel titles
assertions.expected: tiledlayout and nexttile used for multi-panel figure with ylabel/xlabel containing units

name: Non-finite satellite position handling
description: Tests robustness: BeiDou data produces NaN satellite positions that must be filtered before receiverposition or lookangles.
prompt: Compute a GPS + GLONASS + BeiDou position from the GODS RINEX sample data. Make sure the solution is robust.

assertions.description: Code runs without error with all 3 constellations including BeiDou NaN rows
Filters rows with non-finite SatellitePosition values using isfinite or equivalent
assertions.expected: isfinite check on SatellitePosition or equivalent NaN/Inf filtering before receiverposition

assertions.description: Checks for non-finite satellite positions using isfinite (skill-prescribed pattern)

name: Per-constellation validation before combining
description: Tests that the agent validates each constellation independently before combining, excluding any that produce unreliable results.
prompt: Process all available constellations from the GODS RINEX data. Validate each constellation independently before combining them, and exclude any that produce unreliable results.

assertions.description: Code runs without error
Per-constellation accuracy, DOP, or position reported for quality assessment
Validation decision documented per constellation with rationale — include all is valid if data supports it
assertions.expected: Each constellation has a documented quality assessment and include/exclude decision

assertions.description: Final combined solution uses only constellations that passed validation
assertions.expected: Combined solution excludes any constellation flagged as unreliable during validation

name: Ground truth comparison with ENU error
description: Tests ground truth workflow: lla2enu conversion, per-axis error reporting, NaN epoch exclusion, and 2D/3D RMS metrics.
prompt: Compute the GPS+Galileo position solution from GODS station RINEX data. The known ground truth is latitude 39.020734, longitude -76.826657, altitude -8.95 m. How accurate is the solution?

assertions.description: Code runs without error
Uses lla2enu for ground truth error computation
Reports 2D or horizontal RMS error
Reports per-axis error (East, North, Up components)

name: Graceful handling of insufficient satellites
description: Tests degraded geometry: 45-degree elevation mask causes ~54% epoch failure. Agent must handle NaN epochs, report failures, and warn about degraded DOP.
prompt: Compute a GPS position from GODS data with an elevation mask of 45 degrees. What happens to the solution?

assertions.description: Code runs without error despite insufficient satellites at many epochs
Uses lookangles for elevation mask filtering (skill-prescribed approach)
Reports valid vs NaN epochs quantitatively — receiverposition returns NaN for <4 sats
Flags HDOP as degraded using skill's threshold (>6 = degraded)
assertions.expected: HDOP reported with explicit quality assessment referencing the 6 or degraded threshold

assertions.description: Warns about or quantifies the percentage of failed epochs — a key insight for 45-deg mask
assertions.expected: Percentage or fraction of epochs that failed/had insufficient satellites explicitly reported

name: C/N0 threshold sweep and quality assessment without ground truth
description: Tests that the agent sweeps C/N0 thresholds, selects the correct quality proxy for a static receiver (scatter RMS, not error vs ground truth), and provides a data-driven recommendation rather than a fixed threshold.
prompt: I have GPS+Galileo RINEX data from the GODS static station. I want to find a good C/N0 threshold for filtering. Compare no filtering, 25 dB-Hz, and 35 dB-Hz. I don't have ground truth. Which threshold should I use and why?

assertions.description: Code runs without error for all three threshold configurations
C/N0 filtering applied to observation timetable before gnssmeasurements, not after
assertions.expected: S1C column filtered on obsData.GPS or gpsObs before calling gnssmeasurements

assertions.description: Uses scatter RMS as quality proxy for this static receiver — not error vs ground truth
assertions.expected: scatter RMS or position scatter computed from mean position, not from a fixed truth reference

assertions.description: Reports HDOP across configurations to assess geometry impact of filtering
Reports valid epoch count at each threshold to flag excessive data loss
Provides a recommendation with rationale — stops increasing threshold when improvement plateaus or epoch loss becomes significant
assertions.expected: Threshold recommendation with reasoning based on RMS trend and epoch count, not an arbitrary fixed value

name: Recovery from wrong or unavailable observation code
description: Tests the skill's gotcha guidance: wrong observation code produces empty measurements. Agent must inspect available codes via rinexinfo and select a valid alternative.
prompt: I'm trying to process Galileo data from the GODS RINEX sample files but gnssmeasurements returns an empty timetable. The observation code I used was C5Q. Help me figure out what's wrong and get a working Galileo position solution.

assertions.description: Final solution code runs without error and produces a non-empty position
Uses rinexinfo to inspect available observation codes in the RINEX file
Identifies that C5Q is not available and selects a valid band-1 code (C1C or C1X) per skill guidance
assertions.expected: Agent identifies C5Q as unavailable/wrong and switches to C1C or C1X for Galileo

assertions.description: Applies atmospheric corrections as required by skill's must-follow rules

name: GLONASS temporal validity handling
description: Tests awareness of GLONASS-specific constraint: orbital validity is +-15 minutes vs +-2 hours for GPS. Agent should process both and handle or warn about this.
prompt: Compute a GPS + GLONASS position from the GODS RINEX sample data. Tell me about any constellation-specific considerations.

assertions.description: Code runs without error with GPS + GLONASS
GLONASS data processed — references GLONASS nav file or .GLONASS field
References GLONASS-specific temporal handling — either time-based filtering or explicit warning about +-15 minute validity
assertions.expected: Evidence of GLONASS temporal validity awareness: filtering nav data by time proximity or warning about 15-minute constraint

name: Skyplot displayed in full-size dedicated figure
description: Tests the must-follow rule: skyplot must never be placed inside a compact tiledlayout tile. The agent must use a separate figure for the skyplot so satellite markers and labels remain readable.
prompt: Using the GODS station RINEX sample data, compute GPS+Galileo positions and visualize the results. I want a satellite skyplot showing which satellites were visible, plus a multi-panel figure with DOP and position time series.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error
Skyplot is in its own dedicated figure call, not inside a tiledlayout
assertions.expected: skyplot called after a standalone figure (not after nexttile or inside a tiledlayout)

assertions.description: Uses skyplot function for satellite visibility
Multi-panel metrics use tiledlayout/nexttile in a separate figure from the skyplot
assertions.expected: tiledlayout and nexttile used for DOP/position panels in a different figure than the skyplot

assertions.description: Skyplot is not placed inside a nexttile call
assertions.expected: nexttile followed by skyplot in the same figure
